option_a: Set log_connections = on + log_disconnections = on + log_line_prefix with client info
option_c: Very useful for auditing and detecting brute-force attempts

question_text: What is the purpose of the track_functions parameter?
option_a: Enables tracking of function call counts and time in pg_stat_user_functions
explanation: track_functions = all / pl / none — useful for profiling PL/pgSQL usage

question_text: Which view shows the current state of all background writer and checkpointer activity?
youtube_english: pg_stat_bgwriter helps tune checkpoint_completion_target and bgwriter settings

question_text: How do you configure PostgreSQL to use a different authentication method for local vs remote connections?
option_a: Use local ... peer / ident / trust + host ... scram-sha-256 / md5 in pg_hba.conf
option_c: pg_hba.conf order matters — first matching line wins

question_text: What is the recommended way to monitor index bloat and decide when to REINDEX?
option_a: Use pgstattuple extension — pgstatindex('index_name') shows avg_leaf_density
mdn_link: pgstattuple gives real bloat numbers — REINDEX when fragmentation is high

question_text: Which parameter controls the maximum number of replication slots that can exist?
explanation: max_replication_slots prevents too many slots from being created

question_text: How do you enable and configure pgaudit extension for detailed session and object auditing?
option_d: pgaudit provides granular audit logging beyond basic log_statement

question_text: What is the purpose of the track_counts parameter?
option_a: Enables collection of row-level statistics (n_tup_ins/upd/del) in pg_stat_all_tables
explanation: track_counts = on is required for autovacuum to make decisions

question_text: Which setting should be tuned when parallel sequential scans are too aggressive?
option_c: Higher costs discourage parallel plans for small/medium tables

question_text: How do you configure PostgreSQL to log the duration and text of all queries that take longer than 1 second?
correct_answer: log_min_duration_statement is efficient for catching slow queries without flooding logs

question_text: What is the main reason to use external poolers like PgBouncer or PgCat instead of application-level pooling only?
option_a: External poolers reduce PostgreSQL resource consumption (memory per backend) and handle spikes better
explanation: External poolers share few PostgreSQL backends among many clients — lower overhead

question_text: Which view shows cumulative statistics about database-wide activity (xact_commit
option_c: pg_stat_database — one row per database with commits
youtube_kannada: pg_stat_database gives high-level health and I/O / temp usage per database

question_text: How do you enable and configure certificate authentication with Common Name (CN) matching the database user?
option_a: hostssl ... clientcert=verify-full + map CN to role if needed with ident map
option_c: verify-full requires client cert CN = PostgreSQL username

question_text: What is the purpose of the pg_stat_progress_analyze view?
option_a: Shows progress of ANALYZE operations: phase
youtube_english: Helps monitor long-running ANALYZE commands on large tables

question_text: Which parameter should be lowered when parallel workers are causing too much CPU contention?
explanation: Reduce parallel_workers or global max_parallel_workers when CPU is saturated

question_text: How do you configure PostgreSQL to log all connection attempts with client IP
option_c: Set log_connections = on + log_line_prefix = '%m [%p] user=%u db=%d host=%h app=%a '
mdn_link: log_connections + good log_line_prefix gives full connection audit trail

question_text: What is the purpose of the track_activities parameter?
option_a: Enables collection of current query text in pg_stat_activity.query
explanation: track_activities = on is required to see current query in pg_stat_activity

question_text: Which setting controls how many background workers can run in parallel for vacuum and index creation?
explanation: max_parallel_maintenance_workers enables parallel VACUUM / CREATE INDEX

question_text: How do you enable logging of statements that cause errors or timeouts?
option_a: Set log_min_error_statement = error (or log) + log_min_duration_statement for timeouts
option_c: log_min_error_statement logs failing queries — very useful for debugging

question_text: What is the main advantage of using pg_auto_failover over traditional repmgr or Patroni?
option_c: automatic failover with built-in monitoring and witness node
mdn_link: pg_auto_failover is easy to set up for small/medium clusters with automatic failover

question_text: Which extension allows you to see live query plans and statistics without EXPLAIN ANALYZE?
explanation: No built-in live plan viewer — use auto_explain or external tools like check_postgres.pl

question_text: How do you configure PostgreSQL to log bind parameter values for slow queries (PostgreSQL 14+)?
option_c: log_parameter_values logs actual values passed to prepared statements

question_text: What is the recommended setting for random_page_cost on modern NVMe SSD storage?
explanation: Lower random_page_cost encourages index usage on fast random I/O devices

question_text: Which parameter controls the maximum number of WAL senders (streaming replication connections) from primary?
explanation: max_wal_senders should be ≥ number of standbys + some spare

question_text: How do you enable and configure the pgaudit extension to log all SELECT statements on a specific table?
option_a: pgaudit.log = 'read' + pgaudit.log_catalog = off + object auditing on table
option_c: pgaudit allows object-level and session-level auditing

question_text: What is the purpose of the track_commit_timestamp parameter?
option_a: Enables tracking commit timestamp in pg_xact_commit_timestamp() function
explanation: track_commit_timestamp allows querying exact commit time of transactions

question_text: Which setting should be increased when parallel index builds are too slow?
explanation: maintenance_work_mem gives more memory for sort/hash during parallel CREATE INDEX

question_text: How do you log the full text of queries that are waiting for locks longer than a certain time?
option_a: Use log_lock_waits = on + deadlock_timeout = 1s (logs queries waiting > deadlock_timeout)
